caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: MSP evidence is a sibling-code proxy.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Role mix is four-digit 5415, not this residual code.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: S4 and S5 demonstrate vendor capabilities, not a 541519 displacement rate.
inputs.pi_dist: No 541519 pricing mix is measured.
inputs.pi_dist: The cited sources do not measure AI-savings pass-through or retention.
inputs.pi_moat: Platform evidence is not a small-firm moat measure.
inputs.t50_years: No NAICS-specific adoption series exists.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: MSP proxy and vendor framing.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence is not proof of no firm-level need.
inputs.active_consolidators: S3 provides valuation ranges but no buyer count.
inputs.buy_mult: MSP benchmark is a scope proxy.
inputs.exit_mult: Expansion requires evidence of MRR and retention.
inputs.pricing_structure: Target-level contract and revenue-mix diligence is required.

Terminal demand is inferred from a disclosed MSP sibling-code proxy because no dominant 541519 subsegment or recurring-accountability revenue share is sourced; a project/resale-heavy target may face more pressured demand.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $279,051 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541519); class <5: 8,483 firms, avg revenue/firm $429,327 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $67,190 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 841 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,781,555 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $278,813 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 505 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,678,558 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $575,694 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 526 firms, avg revenue/firm $10,815,287 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,692,592 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 179 firms, avg revenue/firm $40,627,151 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,358,149 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 116 firms, avg revenue/firm $190,265,517 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $29,776,553 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 705

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: MSP tooling and bounded vendor-native provisioning and DR examples support automation of repetitive technical and administrative tasks, but physical deployment, recovery accountability, and escalation remain.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541519 ($19,543,540,823) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541519 ($42,029,844,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Publishable with caveats. All mechanics and exact-artifact checks pass, every registered source is accessible, and the sources materially support their bounded capability, MSP-demand, and valuation claims. The selected estimates remain useful under the v3.1.2 text-first contract because their substantial 541519 scope, adoption, capture, owner, consolidator, and revenue-mix uncertainty is explicit. Factor direction is coherent, terminal demand is separated from capture, and no thesis-changing double count or material unsupported contradiction is established.

sources.title: Kaseya 2025 MSP benchmark growth trends
sources.what_it_supports: MSP survey evidence that AI removes repetitive tasks and that revenue is growing in security, cloud management, and infrastructure support.

sources.title: ConnectWise PSA powered by Asio and Pro launch
sources.what_it_supports: Vendor packaging of AI automation across MSP PSA, RMM, security, and data-protection operations.

sources.title: Mayfaire Row B2B professional services acquisition guide
sources.what_it_supports: MSP multiple ranges, the premium for retention and recurring service revenue, and the guide's statement that managed-IT demand is structurally growing because of cybersecurity, cloud adoption, and regulation.

sources.title: Deploy Windows devices using Windows Autopilot
sources.what_it_supports: A bounded vendor documentation example of device deployment without traditional imaging, including profile assignment, monitoring, and troubleshooting.

sources.title: About Azure Site Recovery
sources.what_it_supports: A bounded vendor-native DR example: Azure Site Recovery manages replication, failover, and failback from a portal, while supporting both Azure and on-premises workloads.

title: Other Computer Related Services
